item.title: JavaScript，地球上最糟糕的编程语言 | JavaScript, the worst programming language on the planet
item.description:
地球上见过的最糟糕的编程语言有很多候选者。 普通的 PHP 文件看起来就像你的猫早上吃完早餐后撒在猫砂盆里的东西。 另一方面，Python 太慢了，你可以自己吃早餐，同时等待一个循环完成数到 7。然而，与JavaScript相比，它们都黯淡无光。 到目前为止，JavaScript 是地球上最糟糕的编程语言！

不要误会我的意思，JavaScript 中有很多很棒的想法。 我喜欢它的动态特性。 我喜欢它的功能。 而且由于它实际上是我们的浏览器接受的唯一内容，不幸的是，在不可预见的未来，我们仍然坚持使用它。 但是，如果您出于某种原因决定要创建自己的编程语言，请帮我一个忙； 学习 JavaScript，然后做一些完全相反的东西。 因为这实际上会保证它会比 JavaScript 好上千亿倍。

我曾经在 Twitter 上回复Brendan Eich说他在 30 天内创建了 JavaScript。 此时他回复纠正我并说“11天” 。 我很想回答； 但是，我把我的想法留给自己...... ;)

事实上，关于 JavaScript，我能说的唯一积极的一点是，它比 VBScript 更好，而且它是一种开放语言，与 SilverLight 和 Adob​​e Flash 不同。 从字面上看，这就是我能够很好地谈论 JavaScript 的地方。

我并不是要抨击布伦丹。 但是，如果您学习 JavaScript，请不要爱上它，因为归根结底，它允许声明的变量“穿过”范围的能力，重新定义“this”的能力，以及它的缺乏null/undefined/ “上帝知道什么”的一致性，是如何创建垃圾编程语言的定义。

前几天，我的搭档给我发了一个链接，指向在后端生成 NodeJS 代码的人。 我认为这个想法是向我展示Aista的竞争对手。 当我解释了几件事时，他的反应是“啊哈！;)”

不，我什至不会链接到他们。 生成 NodeJS 代码作为自己手动创建 HTTP REST API 后端的替代方案，基本上与发明一台机器相同，该机器将猫砂均匀地涂抹在客厅的所有墙壁上。 坦率地说，我不想让孩子相信这是一种生成后端 API 的可持续方式。 如果你想使用一种真正的编程语言，它允许你生成真正的后端 API，你可以在下面找到我们的东西。 它之所以是生成 Web API 的唯一可持续方式，是因为不幸的是，它是世界上唯一的元编程语言。 现在你知道了……^_^

  • 在几秒钟内生成 CRUD 应用程序

因为，毫无疑问； JavaScript is the worst programming language on the planet ! JavaScript 是地球上最糟糕的编程语言！



原文:

There are quite a few candidates for the worst programming language the Earth has ever seen. C++ is arguably software development schizophrenia. Java, its managed counterpart, with its rubbish exception handling, literally turns "encapsulation into an unproven theory". The average PHP file looks like something your cat sprinkled in its litter box in the morning after eating its breakfast. And Python on the other hand, is so slow you can literally eat breakfast yourself, while waiting for a loop to finish counting to 7. However, they're all bleak in comparison to JavaScript. JavaScript is the worst programming language on the planet, by far!

Don't get me wrong, there's a lot of great ideas in JavaScript. I love its dynamic nature. I adore its functional abilities. And since it's literally the only thing our browsers accepts, we're unfortunately stuck with it for the unforeseeable future. However, if you for some reason decide that you want to create your own programming language, do me a favour; Study JavaScript, and just make something completely opposite of it. Because that would literally create guarantees of that it would become a bajillion times better than JavaScript.

JavaScript is basically Lisp with brain damage

I once at replied Brendan Eich on Twitter saying he created JavaScript in 30 days. At which point he replied to correct me and said "11 days". I was tempted to reply; "That explains a lot of things". However, I kept my thoughts to myself ... ;)

In fact, the only positive thing I can say about JavaScript, is that it was better than VBScript, and that it was an open language, contrary to SilverLight and Adobe Flash. That's literally where my ability to speak nicely about JavaScript ends.

I don't mean to bash on Brendan. Brendan was one of the original heroes from the browser wars, and I personally know a lot of his colleagues, and he did great stuff with the allocated resources at his disposal back then. However, if you study JavaScript, please do not fall in love with it, because at the end of the day, its ability to allow declared variables to "fall through" scopes, its abilities to re-defined "this", and its lack of consistency on null/undefined/"God knows what", is the very definition of how to create a garbage programming language.

My partner sent me a link to somebody generating NodeJS code on the backend the other day. I think the idea was to show me a competitor to Aista. When I had explained a couple of things, his reaction was "Ahaa! ;)"

And no, I will not even link to them. Generating NodeJS code as an alternative to manually creating your HTTP REST API backend yourself, is basically the same as inventing a machine that spreads your cat's litter evenly applied to all walls in your living room. And I quite frankly don't want to have children believing that this is a sustainable way to generating backend APIs. If you want to play with a real programming language, that allows you to generate real backend APIs, you can find our stuff below. The reason why it's the only sustainable way to generating web APIs, is because unfortunately it's the only meta programming language in the world. Now you know ... ^_^

  • Generate CRUD apps in seconds

Because, have no doubt; JavaScript is the worst programming language on the planet!
item.author: polterguy

item.title: 使用 Isolates 防止 ui Jank | 颤振多处理 | Use Isolates to prevent ui Jank | Flutter multiprocessing
item.description:
拉米罗 - 拉姆根

发表于正如您可能知道的那样，flutter 在通常称为主线程以及 UI 运行的单个线程上异步运行，现在当您需要运行繁重的计算功能时，您会发现您的应用程序因为忙于该功能而冻结和卡顿。

这是 Isolates 发挥作用的地方，这是一种在 dart/flutter 中生成线程并防止主线程停止的方法。

隔离有点挑剔，但是当情况出现时它们很有用，这是完美的情况，我需要加载图像并将其发送到 API 进行处理，这会干扰主线程，但可以在隔离上完成防止这种情况。

让我们看看将隔离与计算功能一起使用的最简单方法之一

运行函数

首先我们需要一个函数来执行，这个函数加载图片并通过http发送给API。

 Future < Uint8List ? > requestImg ( RequestInputs reqInputs ) async { File file = File ( reqInputs . filePath ! ); final http . MultipartRequest request = http . MultipartRequest ( 'POST' , Uri . parse ( 'server:uri' ), ); final Map < String , String > headers = < String , String >{ 'Content-type' : 'multipart/form-data' }; final img_proc . Image tmp = img_proc . decodeImage ( await file . readAsBytes ()) ! ; final List < int > tosend = img_proc . encodeJpg ( tmp ); request . files . add ( http . MultipartFile . fromBytes ( 'file' , tosend , filename: 'aism_source' , contentType: MediaType ( 'image' , 'jpeg' ), )); request . headers . addAll ( headers ); request . fields . addAll ( reqInputs . settings ); final http . StreamedResponse res = await request . send (); debugPrint ( 'Request settings sent' ); Uint8List responseImage = await res . stream . toBytes (); return responseImage ; }

现在我的函数需要多个参数，但计算函数只接受一个函数，为了解决这个问题，我使用自定义类来传递所有数据。

 class RequestInputs { final String ? filePath ; final Map < String , String > settings ; RequestInputs ( this . filePath , this . externalPath , this . settings ); }

计算功能

太好了，我们有了函数和自定义类，让我们看看如何使用计算在隔离上运行它。

 // #1 compute < StickerRequestInputs , Uint8List ? >( requestImg , StickerRequestInputs ( imgPath , storage , settings , ), // #2 ) . then (( value ) = > setState (() { loading = false ; if ( value != null ) { imgBytes = value ; saveImage ( value ); } else { debugPrint ( 'Error!' ); } }));

我们首先需要在计算语句中声明函数的输入和输出类型 #1 compute<Q,R> Q 是输入类型，R 是返回类型。

然后我们传递函数和输入来运行，在这种情况下，我们实例化我们声明的自定义类。

计算是返回类型 R #2 的未来，我们可以使用 async 或 .then 在完成后执行某些操作，在这种情况下，我们使用新数据更新状态，或者如果失败则打印错误。

观看视频以了解所有代码的演练，订阅以查看应用程序的进度：D

好吧，就是这样，我将继续与隔离物合作，看看我们能用它们做什么，但正如我所说，无论在你需要它们时它们是一个不错的选择，使用它们都有点棘手。

一旦暂停，ramgendeploy 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ramgendeploy 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ramgendeploy 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ramgendeploy 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 ramgendeploy：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ramgendeploy 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ramiro - Ramgen

Posted on Aug 21

As you might know flutter runs asynchronously on a single thread often called the main thread and also where the UI is running, now when you need to run heavy computational function you find that you app freezes and janks because is busy with that function.

Here is where Isolates come into action, this is a way to spawn threads in dart/flutter and prevent the main thread to halt.

Isolates are a little finicky but they are useful when the situation comes and this was the perfect situation, I needed to load an image and send it to an API for processing, this was janking the main thread but it can be done on an isolate and prevent that.

Let's see one of the easiest ways of using isolates with the compute function

Function to run

First we need a function to execute, this function loads the image and sends it with http to the API.

Future<Uint8List?> requestImg(RequestInputs reqInputs) async { File file = File(reqInputs.filePath!); final http.MultipartRequest request = http.MultipartRequest( 'POST', Uri.parse('server:uri'), ); final Map<String, String> headers = <String, String>{'Content-type': 'multipart/form-data'}; final img_proc.Image tmp = img_proc.decodeImage(await file.readAsBytes())!; final List<int> tosend = img_proc.encodeJpg(tmp); request.files.add(http.MultipartFile.fromBytes( 'file', tosend, filename: 'aism_source', contentType: MediaType('image', 'jpeg'), )); request.headers.addAll(headers); request.fields.addAll(reqInputs.settings); final http.StreamedResponse res = await request.send(); debugPrint('Request settings sent'); Uint8List responseImage = await res.stream.toBytes(); return responseImage;
}

Now my function needed more than one argument but the compute function only accepts functions with one, to solve this I use a custom class to pass all of the data.

class RequestInputs { final String? filePath; final Map<String, String> settings; RequestInputs(this.filePath, this.externalPath, this.settings);
}

Compute function

Great we have the function and the custom class let's see how to use compute to run it on an isolate.

// #1
compute<StickerRequestInputs, Uint8List?>( requestImg, StickerRequestInputs( imgPath, storage, settings, ),
// #2
).then((value) => setState(() { loading = false; if (value != null) { imgBytes = value; saveImage(value); } else { debugPrint('Error!'); } }));

We first need to declare the function input and output types in the compute statement #1 compute<Q,R> Q is the input type and R is the return type.

Then we pass the function and the input to run, in this case we instantiate the custom class that we declared.

The compute is a future of the return type R #2, we can use either async or .then to do something after it finishes, in this case we update the state with the new data or we print an error if it fails.

Watch the video for a walkthrough of all the code, subscribe to see the progress of the app :D

Alright and that's it, I will continue working with isolates and see what we can do with them, but as i said the are a little tricky to work with regardless they are a good option when you need them.

Once suspended, ramgendeploy will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ramgendeploy will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ramgendeploy will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

item.title: 在开始之前你需要知道多少？ | How much do you need to know before starting?
item.description:
我在思考在学习 React 函数式组件之前是否需要了解 React 类组件。

关于我的一点背景：我在一个内部部门工作，通常被称为“影子 IT”，因为它不是官方 IT 的一部分。

这一点很重要，因为我认识的大多数人来自与计算机科学（和类似）不同的背景。 我本人并非来自 CS 背景。

由于某些原因，在我工作的地方，我们不能只雇佣人，我们有，有点，挑选其他领域的人来和我们一起工作，这通常意味着人们几乎没有或没有编程经验（即使他们真的想要至）。

我之前已经对此提出了一些想法（查看我的其他帖子），但是这一次，我讨论了在学习 React 函数组件之前是否需要学习 React 类组件。

这是一个古老的讨论

这与 React 无关。 仅在前端，我们就有 Vue2 和 Vue3，还有 AngularJS 和 Angular 2+，还有 jQuery、lodash 和 moment.js 的时代。

这也与前端或框架无关。 我们在 ECMAScript 5 之前和之后都有 python2、python3 和 JS。

这只是我的首要考虑，还有很多我自己不知道或没有经验的。 你需要知道多少？

对于初学者，我的观点是你不需要知道任何东西，只要知道那里最新鲜的东西。

当我学习 React 时，我知道类组件的存在，仅此而已。 功能组件足以满足我所需要的一切，即使在今天，据我所知，您只需要针对一种情况（错误边界）的类组件，即使这样，也有一些包可以巧妙地将其抽象为功能。

更重要的是，如果您现在开始，与选择从过时或小众语言/框架开始相比，您将拥有多个使用最新版本的资源，并且有很多人可以一起学习。

想象一下今天尝试将 COBOL 作为第一语言学习？

对于 React，问题当然是遗留问题。 在 React 中，您可以将遗留部分与新代码放在一起。 在其他语言/框架中，您将只有遗留或以某种方式隔离在一小部分或微服务中。

遗留堆栈的开销成本

对于 React，根据您的源代码和遗留代码，您会将新手暴露给类组件、Redux，可能还有 moment 和 lodash。

当时一个完全有效的堆栈，可能散布着功能组件、钩子和不同版本的 ECMAScript 函数，具体取决于它的编写时间。

所有这些都是在实际使用“当前”堆栈并学习所有业务规则和项目结构之前。

与此同时，今天，您可以从功能组件、钩子（包括 Context API）、一些基本的 JS、业务规则和项目结构开始，并随时处理您可能需要的任何其他内容。

基础很重要，只是现在不重要。

一个人可能会比另一个人更早地开始实际编码，并且没有很多不必要的先入之见，如果幸运的话，只会混合并偶然发现他们学到的很多东西……也就是说，如果他们之前没有放弃的话那。

没有必要“欣赏”之前的一切，否则，您需要从汇编开始，用 C 编写所有数据结构和算法，并用 Java 编写所有设计模式，最后在JS。

类型、数据结构、算法、设计模式，以及你今天使用的旧版本的框架、语言和包……这些都是后来出现的，而且可能伴随着问题。

多少钱开始？

忽略除必需品之外的所有内容并使其发挥作用。 要求评论并使其正确。

多学习一些，让它变得更好。



原文:

I was pondering whether you need to know about React Class Components before learning React Functional Components.

A little background about me: I work in an internal department, commonly called “shadow IT” because it’s not part of the official IT.

This is important to say because most people I’ve known came from different backgrounds than that in Computer Science (and similar). I, myself, didn’t come from a CS background.

And because of reasons, where I work we can’t just hire people, we have, kind of, pick people in other areas to come work with us and this usually means people with little to no experience in programming (even when they actually want to).

I’ve already put some of my thoughts on this before (check my other posts), but this time, I got into a discussion about whether you need or not to learn React Class Components before learning React Functional components.

This is an old discussion

This is not about React. In the frontend alone we had Vue2 and Vue3 also AngularJS, and Angular 2+, also the era of jQuery, lodash, and moment.js.

This is also not about frontend or frameworks. We had python2 and python3 and JS before and after ECMAScript 5.

This is just the top of my mind and there are many more that I myself have no idea or experience with. (Examples are welcomed in the comments!)

How much do you need to know about what was?

For a beginner, my opinion is that you don’t need to know anything but whatever is freshest out there.

When I learned React, I knew class components existed and that was it. Functional components were enough for everything I needed and even today, as far as I’m aware you need class components for one case only (Error Boundary), and even then there are packages that neatly abstract that into functional.

More than that, if you’re starting now, you would have multiple sources using the latest versions and a lot of people to learn with along than if you choose to start in an outdated or niche language/framework.

Imagine trying to learn COBOL as a first language today?

In the case of React, the problem is, of course, legacy. In React, you can have the legacy part side by side with the new code. In other languages/frameworks, you would have only the legacy or it somehow segregated in a small part or microservice.

The overhead cost of the legacy stack

In the case of React, depending on your source and your legacy code, you would expose newbies to class components, Redux, and probably moment and lodash.

A totally valid stack back then, probably sprinkled with functional components, hooks, and a different version of ECMAScript functions being used depending on when it was written.

All of that before actually having to use the “current” stack and learn all the business rules and project structure.

Meanwhile today, you can start with functional components, hooks (including Context API), some basic JS, the business rules and project structure, and you tackle anything else you might need as you go.

The basics are important, just not now.

One would probably start to actually code a lot sooner and without a lot of unneeded preconceptions than the other one, that with luck, would only mix and stumble on a lot of things that they learned… that is if they didn’t give up before that.

There’s no need to “appreciate” all that came before, otherwise, you would need to start in Assembly, code all the Data Structures and Algorithms in C, and do all the Design Patterns in Java to finally do a “Hello, World” in JS.

Types, Data Structures, Algorithms, Design Patterns, and the older version of the framework, language, and packages you use today… that comes later and probably with a problem attached to it.

How much to start out?

Ignore all but the essentials and make it work. Ask for reviews and make it right.

Learn some more and make it better.
item.author: noriller

item.title: 数据工程 101：数据工程简介 | Data Engineering 101: Introduction to Data Engineering
item.description:
加图鲁_M

发表于数据工程是构建数据管道并使高质量数据可用于有效的数据驱动决策的过程。
执行这些活动的人称为数据工程师。

但究竟什么是数据管道......
在数据处理中，存在从点 A 到 B 到 C 的数据流，即从应用程序到数据仓库或从数据源到数据库。 这一系列处理步骤称为数据管道。
在这一系列步骤中，每个步骤都会提供一个输出，该输出是下一步的输入。 这一直持续到管道完成。 但是，在某些情况下，独立的步骤可能会并行运行。

数据分析师和数据工程师有什么区别？
数据科学家和数据分析师分析数据集以获得知识和见解。 另一方面，数据工程师构建用于收集、验证和准备高质量数据的系统，然后数据科学家使用这些数据来促进更好的业务决策。

话虽如此，这些是2022 年成为数据工程师所需的一些基本技能

  • 数据结构
  • 了解数据湖和数据仓库
  • 大数据 - Hadoop、Apache Spark(PySpark)、Hive 和 Apache Kafka
  • 云服务 - AWS、Microsoft Azure、Google Cloud、Snowflake 等。
  • 可视化 - Tableau、PowerBI、Looker、Qlikview 等。

祝你在选择追求这段旅程时一切顺利。

谢谢阅读！
任何问题？ 在下方留下您的评论，开始精彩的讨论！

一旦暂停，gathurum 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，gathurum 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，gathurum 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 gathurum 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 gathurum：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 gathurum 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Gathuru_M

Posted on Aug 21

Data Engineering is the process of building data pipelines and making quality data available for efficient data-driven decision-making.
A person who performs these activities is called a Data Engineer.

But what are data pipelines exactly...
In data processing, there is the flow of data from say a point A to B to C i.e., from an application to a data warehouse or from a data source to the database. This series of processing steps is called a data pipeline.
In these series of steps, each step delivers an output that is the input to the next step. This continues until the pipeline is complete. However, in some cases, independent steps may be run in parallel.

What’s the difference between a data analyst and a data engineer?
Data scientists and data analysts analyze data sets to gain knowledge and insights. Data engineers on the other hand build systems for collecting, validating, and preparing that high-quality data which is then used by data scientists to promote better business decisions.

With that said, these are some of the Essential skills required to be a Data Engineer in 2022

  • Data Structures
  • SQL
  • NoSQL
  • Understanding of Data Lakes and Data Warehouse
  • Python
  • Big Data - Hadoop, Apache Spark(PySpark), Hive, and Apache Kafka
  • Cloud Services - AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, Snowflake, etc.
  • Visualization - Tableau, PowerBI, Looker, Qlikview, etc.

I wish you all the best as you choose to pursue this journey.

Thanks for reading!
Any questions? Leave your comment below to start fantastic discussions!

Adheeban Manoharan - Jul 24

item.title: 如何使用 Laravel Sail 创建测试数据库 | How to create a test database with Laravel Sail
item.description:
我从事过要求生产和测试数据库相同的项目。

这可能是因为某些功能在 MariaDB 中有效，但在 SQLite 中无效。 或者一些错误出现在 MySQL 中，但没有出现在 PostgreSQL 中。

当您在一个大型项目中工作时，您需要知道测试数据库的工作方式与生产中的数据库完全相同。
由于测试数据库不同，您不能允许错误出现在生产中。

注意：如果项目很小，您可以使用 SQLite 进行测试。

今天，我将指导你在你当前的数据库服务器中设置一个测试数据库，这要感谢 Laravel Sail。

在我们开始之前

本教程使用 PostgreSQL 作为数据库。
但这个想法对于任何其他数据库都是一样的：在您当前的数据库服务器中创建一个单独的数据库。

前言

要求

数据库设置

我们将使用docker-compose.yml文件中声明的数据库服务器。 在这种情况下，PostgreSQL。 

 services : pgsql : image : ' postgres:14'

一、进入PostgreSQL的CLI

注意：如果你使用 MySQL，你可以使用sail mysql 。

现在，创建一个新数据库

此数据库与您的应用程序使用的默认数据库是分开的。

现在，修改phpunit.xml文件以使用新的数据库和数据库服务器。 最终应该类似于：

 <php> <env name= "DB_CONNECTION" value= "pgsql" /> <env name= "DB_DATABASE" value= "testing" /> </php>

运行测试

现在，您可以运行测试并查看它们是否有效。

它应该类似于：

 PASS Tests \U nit \E xampleTest ✓ example Tests: 1 passed Time: 0.7s

结尾

就是这样。
这是一次性设置，就像一个魅力。

自我推销

如果你觉得这很有用，那么你应该关注我，我会发布更多有趣的内容！ 结论

恭喜，今天你已经学会了如何在 Laravel Sail 中创建一个测试数据库。
无需修改docker-compose.yml文件或创建更多服务。

请在评论中告诉我该教程是否对您有用！

安东尼·德拉韦基亚—— 一旦暂停，alejandroakbal 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，alejandroakbal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，alejandroakbal 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 alejandroakbal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 alejandroakbal 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 alejandroakbal 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I have worked on projects that required the production and testing database to be the same.

This could be because some features work in MariaDB, but not in SQLite. Or some bugs appear in MySQL, but not in PostgreSQL.

When you're working in a large project, you need to know that the testing database works exactly like the one in production.
You can't allow a bug to appear in production because the test database was different.

Note: If the project is small, chances are you are fine using SQLite for testing.

Today, I'm going to guide you through setting up a testing database in your current database server, thanks to Laravel Sail.

Before we start

This tutorial uses PostgreSQL as the database.
But the idea is the same for any other database: create a separate database in your current database server.

Preface

Requirements

Database setup

We will use the database server declared in the docker-compose.yml file. In this case, PostgreSQL.

services: pgsql: image: 'postgres:14'

First, enter PostgreSQL's CLI

Note: If you are using MySQL, you can use sail mysql instead.

Now, create a new database

This database is separated from the default database that your application uses.

PHPUnit configuration

Now, modify the phpunit.xml file to use the new database and database server. Should end up similar to this:

<php> <env name="DB_CONNECTION" value="pgsql"/> <env name="DB_DATABASE" value="testing"/>
</php>

Run the tests

Now, you can run the tests and see if they work.

It should look similar to this:

 PASS Tests\Unit\ExampleTest
  ✓ example

  Tests:  1 passed
  Time:   0.7s

End

That was it.
It's a one-time setup, that works like a charm.

Self-promotion

If you have found this useful, then you should follow me, I will be posting more interesting content! 🥰

Conclusion

Congratulations, today you have learned how to create a test database in Laravel Sail.
Without needing to modify the docker-compose.yml file or creating more services.

Let me know if the tutorial was useful to you in the comments!

Anthony Dellavecchia - Aug 10

开发者；

  • 要点（GitHub）。 社会邮资

  • 领英。

  • 比特桶。

    • 播客

    锚;

    São muitas opções e diversas plataformas disponíveis, né? São muitas opções e diversas plataformas disponíveis, né? Mas pode respirar fundo que também existe um lugar onde você pode reunir e organizar todos os conteúdos que você produz, caso você não esteja usando algum blog ou site pessoal. Mas pode respirar fundo que também existe um lugar onde você pode reunir e organizar todos os conteúdos que você produz, caso você não esteja usando algum blog ou site pessoal。

    Você já ouviu falar do Polywork? Polywork 的声音？ Este é o meu perfil lá: link . Este é o meu perfil lá：链接。 É um site que criar uma rede profissional na qual as pessoas podem compartilhar os destaques de tudo o que elas tem feito ou contribuído, aumentando seu networking. Éum site que criar uma rede profissional na qual as pessoas podem compartilhar os destaques de tudo o que elas feito ou contribuído, aumentando seu networking。 Você pode conhecer mais clicando aqui . Você pode conhecer mais clicando aqui 。

    Caso queira fazer parte da rede, fique à vontade para utilizar meu link clicando aqui . Caso queira fazer parte da rede, fique à vontade para utilizar meu 链接 clicando aqui 。 Ou utilizando o código diretamente: morgannadev-wigglytuff. Ou utilizando o código diretamente: morgannadev-wigglytuff。

    E não se preocupe, o site é gratuito! E não se preocupe, o site é é gratuito！

    E aí, que outras formas você conhece de ajudar comunidades de tecnologia? E aí, que outras formas você conhece de ajudar comunidades de tecnologia? Quais experiências você já teve? Quais experiências você já teve? Compartilha conosco! Compartilha conosco！

    Obrigada por ter lido o artigo e deixo o canal aberto para troca de ideias e feedbacks. Obrigada port ter lido o artigo e deixo o canal aberto para troca de ideias e 反馈。



    原文:

    Você sabia que existem diversas formas de contribuir com comunidades de tecnologia e variados formatos e tipos de produção de conteúdo?

    Se você sempre teve vontade de começar, talvez acabe encontrando aqui uma forma que melhor se encaixa no que você pretende fazer, ou até mesmo o que te deixa mais confortável para contribuir.

    Neste artigo, trago alguns exemplos de produção de conteúdo e alguns exemplos de plataformas que você pode utilizar para te apoiar. Mas existem muitas outras, então fique à vontade para comentar e enriquecer este conteúdo com as suas experiências.

    Não precisa produzir conteúdo para ajudar a comunidade

    Sabia disso? As comunidades de tecnologia não precisam apenas de produção de conteúdo. Talvez você possa ajudar tirando dúvidas das pessoas, apoiando na divulgação da própria comunidade ou dos conteúdos de quem os produz, incentivando pessoas a conhecerem a área e muito mais.

    Não sinta que é uma obrigação sua criar algo. Existem pessoas que se sentem muito melhor ajudando na organização de um evento e na moderação do discord, por exemplo, que na frente fazendo palestras ou escrevendo artigos. E tudo bem. A comunidade também precisa dessas pessoas que cuidam dos bastidores.

    Live coding

    Você sabe o que é live coding? Aqui, neste link, vou deixar um artigo no qual falei sobre isso. Fique à vontade para conhecer mais deste assunto. Mas é uma das formas de contribuir.

    E existem algumas plataformas que você pode fazer live:

    Vídeos

    Algumas pessoas preferem compartilhar conhecimento por vídeos editados, porque isso deixa mais confortável pela possibilidade de edição, diferente da live que tudo o que acontece, de certo e de errado, fica ali registrado para quem estiver acompanhando.

    Algumas plataformas que você pode produzir e postar vídeos:

    Artigos e tutoriais

    Escrever também é uma ótima forma de exercitar sua comunicação e também de compartilhar algum conhecimento. E esses artigos podem ser contando uma experiência que você viveu, algum problema que você aprendeu a solucionar, alguma dica interessante, ou até mesmo em formato de tutoriais ensinando como fazer algo. São materiais bem úteis.

    Existem alguns lugares que você pode postar seus artigos:

    • Dev.to;

    • Blog em plataformas como o WordPress;

    • Blogs pessoais que você pode desenvolver em alguma linguagem de programação;

    • Gist (GitHub). Já vi algumas pessoas compartilhando alguns conteúdos que envolvem código dessa forma também.

    Postagens nas redes sociais

    Muitas pessoas costumam também dar dicas sobre carreira e conteúdos de tecnologia compartilhando via postagens em redes sociais.

    Algumas redes sociais que costumo ver o pessoal compartilhando:

    • Twitter;

    • Instagram;

    • LinkedIn.

    Apoio em fóruns de dúvidas

    Uma excelente maneira de ajudar a comunidade é tirando dúvidas quando sabemos como ajudar em alguns fóruns.

    Alguns deles podem ser encontrados em:

    Projetos de código aberto

    Caso você prefira desenvolver, ou ajudar em documentação, uma boa opção é criar projetos de código aberto (open source) ou ajudar em projetos que já existem, mandando correções ou sugestões de melhoria pelas issues e pull requests.

    Repositórios de projetos de código aberto podem ser encontrados em:

    • GitHub;

    • GitLab;

    • Bitbucket.

    Palestras em eventos

    Alguns eventos podem fazer convites diretamente para as pessoas ou então abrir call for papers para você enviar sua proposta de conteúdo para ser apresentada.

    Para entender um pouco mais sobre o que é e como funciona call for papers, sugiro acompanhar este artigo que escrevi clicando neste link.

    E sua palestra não precisa necessariamente ser algum assunto muito técnico ou avançado. Muitas vezes um conteúdo para pessoas iniciantes ou contando sua experiência pode ajudar muito mais. Tudo depende do objetivo do evento no qual você vai participar.

    Podcast

    Existem diversos formatos de podcasts que podem ser feitos, como entrevistas, contando histórias, compartilhando conhecimento, entre muitos outros.

    Podcasts podem ser encontrados ou postados em diversas plataformas, seguem algumas:

    • Anchor;

    • Spotify;

    • Google Podcast.

    E como eu reúno tudo isso?

    São muitas opções e diversas plataformas disponíveis, né? Mas pode respirar fundo que também existe um lugar onde você pode reunir e organizar todos os conteúdos que você produz, caso você não esteja usando algum blog ou site pessoal.

    Você já ouviu falar do Polywork? Este é o meu perfil lá: link. É um site que criar uma rede profissional na qual as pessoas podem compartilhar os destaques de tudo o que elas tem feito ou contribuído, aumentando seu networking. Você pode conhecer mais clicando aqui.

    Caso queira fazer parte da rede, fique à vontade para utilizar meu link clicando aqui. Ou utilizando o código diretamente: morgannadev-wigglytuff.

    E não se preocupe, o site é gratuito!

    E aí, que outras formas você conhece de ajudar comunidades de tecnologia? Quais experiências você já teve? Compartilha conosco!

    Obrigada por ter lido o artigo e deixo o canal aberto para troca de ideias e feedbacks.

item.title: 时间管理 | 只是牺牲一件事而不是另一件事吗？ | Time Management | Is is just sacrificing one thing over the other?
item.description:
赛富拉·乌斯马尼

发表于嗨，我叫赛富拉·乌斯马尼。 我是一名自由程序员。 我已经编程将近 5 年了。 我为我的客户制作网站（通常是 SaaS）。

我的期中考试才刚刚开始。 我有 3 个 SAAS 项目和大量要研究的东西。 我必须同时保存成绩并在客户面前保持我的声誉。 这两件事对我来说同样重要。 我也必须做生活中的基本事情。 我要睡得好，我要锻炼身体，花一些时间和家人在一起，最重要的是也和自己在一起，以稳定头脑中的所有混乱。 突然间我开始做很多事情，我觉得我的心很痛。 我试图满足每一个期望。 我会努力学习，成为班上的佼佼者。 我将制作我的客户从未见过的 SAAS，他们会立即说 WOW。 我会做前所未有的运动，我会变成我梦想中的自己。 我将学习生产力、赚钱、个人发展书籍等等。 但是单单学习就占用了我所有的时间，因为我不是很擅长，所以我必须花更多的时间来变得更好。 这些主题对我来说是不同的和新的。 所以我不能做所有其他的事情，这会导致紧张。 我必须管理我的时间。 但究竟什么是时间管理？ 我必须在 24 小时内完成所有工作吗？ 我可以在 24 小时内完成吗？ 理论上，是的！ 实际上，不是吗？ 所以我得出结论，有些东西会被牺牲。 我必须放弃完美，跳过一些事情来腾出时间。 时间管理不适用于太多事情。 它打破了。 它变成了时间分配。 将时间分配给不同的日子，而是将时间分配给一周中的不同日子。

每周锻炼 2 次，而不是每天锻炼 5 次。 少出门，多做重要项目。 从客户那里花更多的时间，沟通。 每天有专门的时间学习。 以不同的时间间隔全天学习。 你不能一次全部学习。 在一天内扩展它。 消耗一整天而不是一天的一部分。 继续努力，因为这段时间也会过去。

我想我明天有考试，所以我最好学习😁
爱你们，很快再见！ 再见❤

跟着我：

雇用我： https ://www.fiverr.com/saifullah02（用于网络开发目的）
我的新博客： saifullahusmani.com
在此处关注我： https ://dev.to/saifullahusmani
不和谐： https ://discord.gg/AKyvy8Y
脸书： https ://www.facebook.com/SaifullahUsmani693

一旦解除暂停，saifullahusmani 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，saifullahusmani 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 saifullahusmani 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 saifullahusmani：

原文:

Saifullah Usmani

Posted on Aug 21

Hi, my name is Saifullah Usmani. I am a freelance Programmer. I have been programming for almost 5 years now. And I make websites (SaaS commonly) for my clients.

My mid-session exams just started. I have 3 SAAS projects and a load of stuff to study. I got to save grades and keep my reputation in front of my clients at the same time. Both of the things are equally important to me. I have to do the basics things in life too. I got to sleep well, I got to do exercise and spend some time with family and most importantly with myself too to stabilize all the chaos in the mind. Suddenly I started doing so many things and I felt my mind hurting. I was trying to meet every expectations. I will study hard and become a topper of my class. I will make the SAAS my clients have never seen and they will say WOW instantly. I will do exercise like never before, I will transform into my dream self. I will study productivity, money making, personal development books and a lot more. But tbh study alone takes all of my time because I am not very good at it so I got to spend more time to get better at it. The subjects are different and new to me. So I cannot do all of the other stuff, this causes tension. I have to manage my time. But what is time management exactly? Do I have to do all of it in 24 hours? Can I do it in 24 hours? Theoretically, Yes! practically, No? So I have concluded that somethings will get sacrificed. I have to let go of perfection and skip some things to make time. Time management doesn't work with too many things. It breaks. It becomes Time Allocation. Allocate time to not different parts of days but rather allocate time to different days of week.

Exercise 2 times a week rather than 5 times or everyday. Go out less, work on important projects more. Take more time from clients, Communicate. Dedicated time of day to study. Study throughout the day on different intervals. You cannot study all at once. Expand it over the day. Consume whole day rather than part of day. Keep working hard as this time will pass away too.

I think I have an exam tomorrow so I better study😁
Love you guys, see ya all soon! Bye❤

item.title: Angular 中的可观察对象 | Observables in Angular
item.description:
帕思舒克拉

发表于此功能在 Angular 中经常使用，因为它负责处理多个值、Javascript 中的异步编程以及事件处理过程。

在这个简单的例子中，我们将调用一个 api 接收一个 Observable，然后 console.log 将其注销。

在此之前，让我们回顾一下两个概念

您可以将 Observable 视为普通数据流的包装器。

它在流完成时发出完成信号，或者在流出错时发出错误信号。

您可以像定义任何其他变量一样定义可观察函数。 只有当有人订阅它时，observable 才会开始发出值。

  1. 什么是观察者/订阅者？

观察者是消费可观察者发出的值的人，这些被称为观察者或订阅者。

观察者必须订阅 observable 才能从 observable 接收值。 在订阅它时，它可以选择传递三个回调。 下一个（），错误（）和完成（）

创建一个 Observables

 obs = new Observable((observer) => { console.log("Observable starts") observer.next("1") observer.next("2") observer.next("3") observer.next("4") observer.next("5") })

订阅它

一旦暂停，parthshukla 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，parthshukla 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，parthshukla 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 parthshukla 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

原文:

Parth Shukla

Posted on Aug 21

Observable in Angular is a feature that provides support for delivering messages between different parts of your single-page application. This feature is frequently used in Angular because it is responsible for handling multiple values, asynchronous programming in Javascript, and also event handling processes.

In this simple example we'll call an api recieve an Observable and just console.log it out.

Just before that let us brush up two concepts

Observable is a function that converts the ordinary stream of data into an observable stream of data. You can think of Observable as a wrapper around the ordinary stream of data.

Observable stream or simple Observable emits the value from the stream asynchronously. It emits the complete signals when the stream completes or an error signal if the stream errors out.

Observables are declarative. You define an observable function just like any other variable. The observable starts to emit values only when someone subscribes to it.

  1. What are observers/subscribers?

The observers are the ones which consumes the value emitted by the observable, these are called observers or subscribers.

The observer must subscribe with the observable to receive the value from the observable. While subscribing it optionally passes the three callbacks. next(), error() & complete()

Creating an Observables

 obs = new Observable((observer) => {
    console.log("Observable starts")
      observer.next("1")
      observer.next("2")
      observer.next("3")
      observer.next("4")
      observer.next("5")
  })

Subscribing to it

this.obs.subscribe({ next: (v) => console.log(v), error: (e) => console.error(e), complete: () => console.info('complete')

})

*Returning an observable and subscribing it during an API call
*

catAPI() {
    return this.http.get<Observable<any>>('https://catfact.ninja/fact')
  }

  callingCatAPI(){
    return this.catAPI().subscribe( val => {
      console.warn(val);
    })
  }

item.title: 数据工程概论 | Introduction to Data Engineering
item.description:
布赖恩·马查里亚

发表于简介 数据无处不在。 近年来，随着连接设备数量的增加及其使用范围的扩大，世界已经产生了大量传统方法无法处理的数据。 根据 Statista 的数据，截至 2021 年，有 238 亿台设备连接到互联网，其中 58% 是物联网设备（智能家居设备、联网自动驾驶汽车、工业设备等），其余 42% 是非物联网设备设备（笔记本电脑、智能手机等）。 这意味着如今的数据可以来自各种来源。 其中一些是： 用户活动：任何用户操作都会产生数据，甚至反映我们在何处以及多久点击我们使用的移动应用程序的数据都是有价值的。 包含此类信息的每条数据都需要保存并分发到应用程序的数据中心。 需要检索所有这些数据并将其发送到数据池以进行进一步分析。 程序日志：当今的计算机应用程序由许多组件组成，所有这些组件都会产生程序日志。 这些日志被发送到数据工程管道以进行进一步分析。 世界产生的所有不同的结构化数据都需要交付给软件结构中的其他组件，或者由业务分析师和数据科学家进行可视化和解释。 然而，这种交付并不是一项简单的任务。 关于如何交付数据有一些关键要求： 数据需要以标准化的结构交付 数据不应存在任何冲突 可能需要删除数据重复 数据需要可靠 必须以最小的延迟完成数据交付因此，这些信息的传递是一个复杂的过程，如果执行不当，可能会产生很大的问题，尤其是对于大中型公司而言。 这就是数据工程师发挥作用的地方。 在本文中，您将学习数据工程的基础知识，包括： 什么是数据工程以及为什么要确保稳健的数据管道 数据科学与数据工程之间的区别 数据工程越来越受欢迎的原因 良好数据的技能工程师 DataCamp 如何帮助您成为数据工程师 数据工程师的职责是什么？ 简而言之，数据工程师： 旨在使数据科学家和商业智能工程师（或任何处理数据的人）可以轻松访问和使用数据 优化公司的大数据系统架构 设计和配置数据库 与商业智能工程师团队合作，数据科学家和数据分析师 探索和转换数据 为了执行所有这些任务，数据工程师首先需要设置 ETL 管道。 数据工程师有责任建立和维护这些管道。 有 3 种类型的 ETL 管道： 提取 由数据源创建的大量数据以不同的格式收集。 转换 由于数据的格式不同且来源不一致，因此对数据进行处理以使其尽可能标准化。 这增加了后续步骤的数据的可发现性和可用性。 加载 一旦数据处于可发现和可用的格式，它通常存储在数据仓库中。 这使得数据随时可供进一步分析。 数据仓库 数据仓库是存储标准化数据的地方。 它们针对过滤和读取大量数据进行了优化。 在现代数据仓库中，结构化和非结构化数据都可以存储，因为存储无法分类的数据也很重要，例如照片和视频。 数据工程与数据科学数据工程师和数据科学家的角色之间的差异很重要。 一方面，数据科学家在公司中发挥着重要作用，因为他们为数据驱动的决策做出了贡献。 然而，数据科学家的成功与他们拥有的数据一样好。 另一方面，数据工程师是设计系统的人，这些系统以有组织和一致的方式向数据科学家提供数据。 一旦数据工程师为他们提供了信息，数据科学家就会尝试使用数据工程师提供的格式在数据中找到模式以得出结论。 数据工程师和数据科学家显然紧密合作。 我们可以把它想象成一场足球比赛。 在足球中，是教练制定了创造进球机会的策略，也是教练将球员安排在了特定的位置上。 在这种情况下，教练是数据工程师，所以我们可以认为球是数据，得分的球员是数据科学家。 团队的其余部分是基础设施。 为什么数据工程越来越受欢迎？ 根据 Burning Glass Nova 平台的研究，“数据工程师”的职位在 2019 年增加了 88.3%。根据这项研究，数据工程师的需求自 2016 年以来一直在上升。另一个有趣的信息是，数据工程师的职位空缺数量几乎是数据科学家职位空缺的 5 倍。 数据工程师的薪水也比数据科学家的薪水高 20-30%。 如上所述，数据科学家是数据处理链中的最后一环。 他们需要数据工程师处理数据才能执行他们的工作。 随着数据源和数据类型的数量成倍增加，对数据工程师的需求也在增长。 出于这个原因，公司最近开始在数据的提取和分发方面进行更多投资。 在图 4.1 中，金字塔图显示了数据科学需求的层次结构。 在这个层次结构中，分配给数据工程师的任务位于金字塔的底部，这说明了数据工程师对公司的重要性。 图 4.1：数据科学需求层次结构。 资料来源：hackernoon 数据工程师的技能是什么？ 数据工程师的技能通常与软件工程师和数据科学家的技能交织在一起，如图 5.1 所示。 图 5.1：数据角色和技能集 来源：RyanSwanstrom 数据工程师的角色更接近软件工程师还是数据科学家？ 一组数据工程师以前的职业如图5.2所示。 根据这个数字，超过 40% 的人具有软件工程背景。 这不仅意味着数据工程和软件工程比我们最初想象的更接近，而且现在的软件工程师比任何其他领域更有可能转向数据工程。 图 5.2：按先前角色划分的数据工程师（前 10 名）。 资料来源：stitchdata 在 DataCamp，我们认为以下是优秀数据工程师的技能： 软件工程背景 数据工程师需要有软件工程背景，因为他们需要用他们的面向对象编程、数据知识来应对数据工程挑战结构和算法。 数据工程师必须具有 Python、Scala 或 Java 方面的经验。 工具 数据工程师需要熟悉 Airflow、Apache 或 Kafka 等数据工程工具。 数据库技术 数据工程师应该具备多种数据库的知识。

云技术 数据工程师应该习惯于使用多种云平台，如亚马逊网络服务、谷歌云平台和微软 Azure。 他们还需要具备云架构和 DevOps 工具方面的经验，因为他们负责系统数据流部分的自动化。

原文:

Brian Macharia

Posted on Aug 21

Introduction Data is everywhere. In recent years, with the growing number of connected devices and the expansion of their use, the world has been producing a vast amount of data that cannot be handled with traditional methods anymore. According to Statista, as of 2021 there are 23.8 billion devices connected to the internet and 58% of those are IoT devices (smart home devices, connected self-driving cars, industrial devices, etc.) and the remaining 42% are non-IoT devices (laptops, smartphones, etc.). This means that nowadays data can come from various sources. Some of these are: User activities: Any user action produces data and even the data that reflects where and how often we tap on the mobile applications we use is valuable. Every piece of data that contains this kind of information needs to be kept and distributed to the applications’ data centers. “Internet of Things” devices: These devices produce a huge amount of information, such as sensor data generated in many different locations. All this data needs to be retrieved and sent to a data pool for further analysis. Program logs: Today’s computer applications consist of many components and all those components produce program logs. These logs are sent to a data engineering pipeline for further analysis. All the different structured data that the world produces needs to be delivered to other components in the software structure, or visualized and interpreted by business analysts and data scientists. However, this delivery is not a simple task. There are some crucial requirements on how to deliver the data: Data needs to be delivered in a standardized structure The data should not present any conflicts Data duplication may need to be removed The data needs to be reliable The data delivery must be done with minimum delay Therefore, the delivery of this information is a complex process and, if it is not carried out properly, it can be very problematic, especially for medium and large-scale companies. This is where data engineers come into play. In this article you will learn the basics of data engineering, which are: What data engineering is and why to ensure a robust data pipeline The difference between data science and data engineering The reason behind the increasing popularity of data engineering The skills of a good data engineer How DataCamp can help you become a data engineer What are the responsibilities of a data engineer? To put it simply, data engineers: Aim to make data easily accessible and available for data scientists and business intelligence engineers (or anyone working with data) Optimize the big data system architecture of companies Design and configure databases Collaborate with teams of business intelligence engineers, data scientists, and data analysts Explore and transform data In order to perform all these tasks, data engineers first need to set up the ETL pipelines. ETL pipelines ETL pipelines receive complex data at regular time intervals and process it with the aim of storing the data in a usable form. It is the responsibility of the data engineers to establish and maintain these pipelines. There are 3 types of ETL pipelines: Extract A vast number of data created by data sources is collected in different formats. Transform Since the data comes in different formats and due to its inconsistent sources, the data is processed to become as standardized as possible. This increases the discoverability and usability of the data for the next steps. Load Once the data is in a discoverable and usable format, it is usually stored in data warehouses. This makes the data available at all times for further analysis. Data warehouses Data warehouses are where standardized data is stored. They are optimized for filtering and reading large volumes of data. In modern data warehouses, both structured and unstructured data can be stored, because it is also important to store the data that cannot be categorized, such as photos and videos. Data engineering vs data science The differences between the roles of a data engineer and a data scientist are important. On the one hand, data scientists have an important role in companies because they contribute to data-driven decision making. Nevertheless, the success of data scientists is only as good as the data that they have available. On the other hand, data engineers are the ones who design the systems that provide data to data scientists in an organized and consistent manner. Once the data engineers make the information available for them, data scientists then try to find patterns in the data to reach conclusions, using the format provided by the data engineers. Data engineers and data scientists clearly work closely together. We can think of it as a football match. In football, it is the coach who develops the strategies that will create goal opportunities, and it is the coach who sets the players in certain positions. In this context the coach is the data engineer, so we can consider that the ball is the data and the player who scores is the data scientist. The rest of the team is the infrastructure. Why has data engineering increased in popularity? According to research conducted on the Burning Glass Nova platform, job postings for "data engineer" increased by 88.3% in 2019. According to this research, demand for data engineers has been on the rise since 2016. Another interesting piece of information is that the number of job openings for data engineers is almost 5 times higher than the job openings for data scientists. The salary of a data engineer is also 20-30% higher than the salary of a data scientist. As stated above, data scientists are the final link in the data processing chain. They need the data to be processed by a data engineer in order to perform their job. As the number of data sources and data types multiply, the need for a data engineer grows. For this reason, companies have recently started to invest more in the extraction and distribution of data. In Figure 4.1, a pyramid chart displays the hierarchy of needs of data science. In this hierarchy, the tasks assigned to data engineers are at the base of the pyramid, which explains how crucial data engineers are for companies. Figure 4.1: The data science hierarchy of needs. Source: hackernoon What are the skills of a data engineer? The skills of data engineers are generally intertwined with the skills of software engineers and data scientists, as shown in Figure 5.1. Figure 5.1: Data roles and skill sets Source: RyanSwanstrom Is the role of a data engineer closer to the one of a software engineer or to the one of a data scientist? The previous occupations of a group of data engineers are displayed in Figure 5.2. According to this figure, over 40% of them have a software engineering background. Not only does this mean that data engineering and software engineering are significantly closer than we may have first thought, but also that software engineers nowadays are much more likely to switch to data engineering than any other field. Figure 5.2: Data engineers by prior role (top 10). Source: stitchdata At DataCamp we believe the following to be the skills of a good data engineer: Software engineering background Data engineers need to have a software engineering background, since they need to approach data engineering challenges with their knowledge of object-oriented programming, data structures and algorithms. Data engineers must be experienced in Python, Scala or Java. Tools Data engineers need to be comfortable with data engineering tools like Airflow, Apache or Kafka. Database Technologies A data engineer should have knowledge of multiple kinds of databases.

Cloud Technologies Data engineers should be comfortable using multiple cloud platforms like Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft Azure. They need to have experience in cloud architecture and DevOps tools as well, since they are responsible for the automation of the data flow part of the system.

Nguewo Fossong Christian - Aug 21

item.title: 数据工程 101：数据工程简介 | Data Engineering 101: Introduction to Data Engineering
item.description:
设计和构建大规模数据收集、存储和分析系统的实践称为数据工程。数据工程，也称为信息工程，是一种基于软件的方法来创建信息系统。 数据工程包括收集、转换和管理来自各种系统的数据。 此过程可确保数据有用且易于访问。 首先，数据工程强调数据收集和分析的实际应用。 调查上述调查需要复杂的解决方案，这不足为奇。 因此，数据工程采用复杂的方法来收集和验证数据，从数据集成工具到人工智能。

同样，数据工程依赖于特殊机制将发现的数据应用于现实世界的场景，通常是通过设计和监控复杂的处理系统。

数据工程师具体做什么？

数据工程师构建的系统可以收集、管理原始数据并将其转换为可用信息，供数据科学家和业务分析师在各种环境中进行解释。 他们的最终目标是使数据可访问，以便企业可以评估和优化其绩效。

数据工程生态系统

为什么选择数据工程

  1. 使用和处理不同的数据格式，以生成用于生产的高质量数据。
  2. 在提取最大值的同时大规模处理大量数据的能力。
  3. 数据管道和流的自动化
  4. 善用元数据。
  5. 从实时数据（质量数据）中获得令人难以置信的见解的能力。
数据工程路线图
  1. 语言-Python、scala、java
  2. 自动化和脚本 - CRON
  3. 数据处理-Spark、数据流-Kafka、流处理-spark
  4. 数据库 - SQL 和 NoSql
  5. 工作流程 - apache 工作流程
  6. 云计算——AWS、Azure、GCP
  7. 基础设施——Docker、kubernetes
数据工程师与数据科学家

简而言之，数据科学家只有在以适当的格式接收数据后才能解释数据。 数据工程师的工作是将数据提供给数据科学家。 因此，截至目前，数据工程师比数据科学家更受欢迎，因为工具无法执行数据工程师的任务

您将通过使数据科学家、分析师和决策者更易于访问，在组织的成功中发挥重要作用。

如果您发现数据工程有趣且有趣，让我们在Github.com/James-Wachuka上进行合作

原文:

The practice of designing and building large-scale data collection, storage, and analysis systems is known as data engineering.Data engineering, also known as information engineering, is a software-based approach to creating information systems. Data engineering includes the collection, transformation, and management of data from various systems. This procedure ensures that data is useful and easy to access. Data engineering, above all, emphasizes the practical applications of data collection and analysis. It should come as no surprise that investigating the aforementioned inquiries necessitates complex solutions. As a result, data engineering employs complex methodologies for gathering and validating data, ranging from data integration tools to artificial intelligence.

Similarly, data engineering relies on special mechanisms to apply discovered data to real-world scenarios, typically by designing and monitoring sophisticated processing systems.

What exactly does a data engineer do?

Data engineers build systems that collect, manage, and convert raw data into usable information for data scientists and business analysts to interpret in a variety of settings. Their ultimate goal is to make data accessible so that businesses can evaluate and optimize their performance.

Data Engineering Ecosystem

1.Data - consists of various data types, formats, and sources. 2.Data stores and repositories — Databases, both relational and non-relational, as well as data warehouses, data marts, data lakes, and big data stores, which store and process data. 3.Data Pipelines — Collect data from multiple sources, clean it, process it, and transform it into data that can be analyzed.

4.Analytics and Data-Driven Decision Making — Make well-processed data available for additional business analytics, visualization, and data-driven decision making.

Why Data Engineering
  1. To work with and process disparate data formats in order to produce high-quality data for use in production.
  2. The ability to work with large amounts of data at scale while extracting maximum value.
  3. Automation of data pipelines and streams
  4. Make good use of meta data.
  5. The ability to derive incredible insights from real-time data ( quality data).
Data Engineering roadmap
  1. Languages-Python, scala, java
  2. Automation and scripting - CRON
  3. data processsing - Spark, data streaming-Kafka, stream processing-spark
  4. Databases - SQL and NoSql
  5. Workflow - apache workflow
  6. Cloud computing- AWS, Azure, GCP
  7. Infrastructure - Docker, kubernetes
Data Engineers Vs Data Scientists

Simply put, the data scientist can interpret data only after receiving it in an appropriate format. The data engineer's job is to get the data to the data scientist. Thus, as of now, data engineers are more in demand than data scientists because tools cannot perform the tasks of a data engineer

🚀A career in this field can be rewarding as well as challenging. You will play an important role in an organization's success by making data scientists, analysts, and decision-makers more accessible.

If you find data engineering interesting and fun, Let's collaborate on Github.com/James-Wachuka

Vladimir Mihailenco - Apr 21

item.title: 命运之轮 | Wheel Of Fortune
item.description:
杰克麦奎尔

发表于一个利用 REDIS 来维护轮盘部分奖品分配和分配的财富之轮项目。

这个项目带有一个 GRPC 服务器、客户端和 GO 内置的管理客户端来维护轮子。

一个使用 GRPC 构建的 VUEJS 网站，ENVOY 的创建是为了旋转虚拟命运之轮！

提交类别：

古怪的通配符

截图！

使用的语言

去，Javascript，LUA

链接到代码

由 GRCP 服务器和客户端公开的由 Redis 支持的模拟命运之轮。

这个怎么运作

本项目模拟命运之轮，利用 Redis 维护轮子的各个部分，即奖品分配和分配

服务器利用 Redis LUA 脚本同步执行用户旋转，并通过 GRPC 公开 API 来维护和旋转和命运之轮！

数据的存储方式：

命运之轮的分段和操作标志存储在 Redis Keys 中。

创造财富之轮

  • 创建轮子时，会生成以下键：
命运之轮部分

对于命运之轮中每个已定义的部分，分配的奖品有一个 REDIS 键，如'wheel:{wheelname}:segment:{index}' ，数据像INCRBY {KEY} {VALUE}一样存储在 TX 中

命运之轮启用状态

要设置启用状态...

其他资源/信息

原文:

Jack McGuire

Posted on Aug 21

A Wheel Of Fortune project that utilises REDIS in order to maintain wheel segment prize allocation and distribution.

This project comes with a GRPC Server, Client and Admin client built in GO to maintain wheels.

A VUEJS website built with GRPC, ENVOY was created to spin the virtual Wheel Of Fortune!

Submission Category:

Wacky Wildcards

Screenshots!

Language Used

GO, Javascript, LUA

Link to Code

A simualted Wheel Of Fortune backed by Redis exposed by a GRCP Server & Client.

How it works

This project simulates a Wheel Of Fortune by making use of Redis to maintain the segments of a wheel i.e. prize allocation and distribution

The server makes use of Redis LUA scripts to synchronously execute user spins and exposes an API via GRPC to maintain and spin and wheels of fortune!

How the data is stored:

Segments and Operational Flags of a wheel of fortune are stored in Redis Keys.

Wheel Of Fortune Creation

  • When a wheel is CREATED the following keys are generated:
wheel of fortune segment(s)

For each defined segment within a wheel of fortune with an allocated prize a REDIS key like 'wheel:{wheelname}:segment:{index}', data is stored like INCRBY {KEY} {VALUE} in a TX

wheel of fortune Enabled Status

To set the enable status…

Additional Resources / Info

  • REDIS for wheel of fortune management
  • GO to expose a GRPC API
  • ENVOY is used to provide a HTTP TO GRPC proxy
  • VUEJS for front-end webapp

Jack McGuire https://github.com/jackmcguire1

item.title: 活动记录关联 | Active Record Associations
item.description:
什么是 Active Record 关联？

让我们通过一个示例来深入了解不同类型的关联。

关联必须以单数形式使用，因为 Rails 会自动从关联名称推断类名称。 在此示例中，每个评论都可以分配给一个用户。 它将在评论表上查找 user_id。

例如，每个供应商只有一个帐户。 该帐户将与supplier_id 关联。

关联必须以复数形式使用。 例如，在此示例中，用户可以有许多评论。

这些模型通过另一个模型连接。 例如，一个游戏通过评论有很多用户，而一个用户通过评论有很多游戏。 评论属于游戏和用户。

表格看起来像这样。

例如，如果一个部门有一个经理，并且每个经理都与一个经理历史相关联，那么它将如下所示：

例如，有一个评论模型，但现在只有一个游戏和用户模型。

还有一个更高级的多态关联，要了解更多信息，我建议访问此。 我希望这是有帮助的，祝你编码好运！

资料来源：
- https://guides.rubyonrails.org/association_basics.html - https://guides.rubyonrails.org/association_basics.html
- https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5mhuNSkV_vQ&t=409s - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5mhuNSkV_vQ&t=409s



原文:

What are Active Record Associations?

Association in Rails defines the relationship between models and it's used to make it easier on coders like you. Let's dive into the different types of associations with a example for each.

The 6 Types of Associations

1.belongs_to
A belongs_to association creates a connection with another model. The associations has to be used in a singular term because Rails will automatically infer the class name from the association name. In this example, each review can be assigned to exactly one user. It will look for a user_id on the reviews table.

2.has_one
A has_one association indicates that one other model has a reference to this model. For example, each supplier only has one account. The account will be associate with supplier_id.

3.has_many
A has_many association is a one-to-many connection with another model. The associations has to be used in a plural term. For example, a user can have many reviews in this example.

4.has_many :through
A has_many :through association has a many-to-many connection with another model. The models are connected through another model. For example, a game has many users, through reviews and a user has many games, through reviews. A review belongs to a game and a user.

The tables will look something like this.

5.has_one :through
A has_one :through association sets a one-to-one connection with another model but can proceed through a third model. For example, if a department has one manager, and each manager is associated with one manager history, then it will look like this:

6.has_and_belongs_to_many
A has_and_belongs_to_many association is similar to the has_many :through association, but there is no third model. For example, there was a review model, but now there is only a game and user model.

There is also a polymorphic association that is more advanced and to learn more about that I would recommend visiting this. I hope this was helpful and good luck coding!

Sources:
-https://guides.rubyonrails.org/association_basics.html
-https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5mhuNSkV_vQ&t=409s
item.author: josephineluu

item.title: DAG 在 Spark 中是如何工作的？ | How does DAG work in Spark?
item.description:
有向无环图（DAG）是一个没有有向环的有限有向图。 有有限多个顶点和边，其中每条边都从一个顶点指向另一个顶点。 这就是为什么它非常适合生成实现基于阶段的调度的多阶段调度层。 由于层数和深度可以超过两个但有限，它比旧的 Map 和 Reduce 程序更好和优化，后者仅在两个阶段运行：Map 和 Reduce。

解释器是第一层，使用 Scala 解释器，Spark 对代码进行一些修改来解释。 当您在 Spark 控制台中输入代码时，Spark 会创建一个运算符图。 当我们在高层调用 Spark RDD 上的 Action 时，Spark 会将算子图提交给 DAG 调度程序。

在 DAG 调度程序中将算子划分为任务的各个阶段。 一个阶段包含基于输入数据分区的任务。 例如，地图操作员在一个阶段进行调度。 这些阶段传递给任务调度程序。 它通过集群管理器启动任务。 任务调度程序不知道阶段的依赖关系。

工人在奴隶上执行任务。 下图简要描述了 DAG 在 Spark 作业执行中的工作步骤：

驱动程序： Apache Spark 引擎调用应用程序的主程序并创建 Spark Context。 集群管理器：集群管理器做资源分配工作。

希望这会有所帮助。



原文:

Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) is a finite directed graph with no directed cycles. There are finitely many vertices and edges, where each edge is directed from one vertex to another. DAG contains a finite set of vertices and edges in sequence. Every edge in DAG is directed from top to bottom in the sequence.

This is why it's great for generating multistage scheduling layers that implement stage-based scheduling. As the number of layers and depth can be more than two but finite, it's better and optimized than the older Map and Reduce programs, which run in two stages only: Map and Reduce.

Working of DAG Scheduler
The interpreter is the first layer, using a Scala interpreter, Spark interprets the code with some modifications. Spark creates an operator graph when you enter your code in the Spark console. When we call an Action on Spark RDD at a high level, Spark submits the operator graph to the DAG scheduler.

Divide the operators into stages of the task in the DAG scheduler. A stage contains tasks based on the partition of the input data. The DAG scheduler pipelines operators together. For example, map operators schedule in a single stage. The stages pass on to the task scheduler. It launches tasks through cluster manager. The dependencies of stages are unknown to the task scheduler.

The workers execute the task on the slave. The following diagram briefly describes the steps of how DAG works in the Spark job execution:

Driver program: The Apache Spark engine calls the main program of an application and creates Spark Context. A Spark Context consists of all the basic functionalities. RDD is created in the Spark Context.

Spark Driver: It contains various other components, such as DAG scheduler, task scheduler, backend scheduler, and block manager, which are responsible for translating the user-written code into jobs that are actually executed on the cluster.

Cluster manager: Cluster manager does the resource allocating work.

Hope this was helpful.

    Marco Biedermann

    Welcome to my Weekly Digest #33.

    Origin trials are a way to test a new or experimental web platform feature. A third-party origin trial makes it possible for providers of embedded content to try out a new feature across multiple sites.

    by Google Chrome Developers

    Getting Creative with Keyframes

    Working with keyframes can be difficult even if you only have one animation, but the real problems begin when you have 3 objects with 5 animations, each of which has different keyframes, duration, timing-function… and yet they all need to be exactly synchronized. So how do we do that?!

    by Amit Sheen

    TypeScript makes JavaScript safer by adding static type definitions. Static definitions are wonderful; they prevent developers from making trivial mistakes by ensuring every assignment and invocation is done correctly.

Depending on the material and the property you're after, there is more or less variability to take into account. Use these values as a starting point, or as validation for your existing materials, and then tweak further using your artistic freedom.




    tsx

    TypeScript Execute (tsx): Node.js enhanced with esbuild to run TypeScript & ESM

    TypeScript Execute (tsx): Node.js enhanced with esbuild to run TypeScript & ESM files

    • Blazing fast on-demand TypeScript & ESM compilation

item.description:
玛莎·穆尼

发表于欢迎来到我的数据工程博客！

我将参加由 Data Science East Africa 和 Lux Academy 举办的为期 12 周的数据工程指导计划，我将在这里写下我的学习进度。

第 1 周：数据工程简介。

数据工程是用于大规模收集、存储和分析数据的系统的设计和开发。

第 1 周是关于熟悉 DE 概念并设置环境。

使用的技术和软件要求。

  • 用于编码的 Python 语言、python IDE 和文本编辑器。 我更喜欢蟒蛇。

  • 第 1 周是关于基本介绍和入门的。

Martha Mueni

Posted on Aug 21

Welcome to my blog on data engineering!

I will be participating in a 12-week data engineering mentoring program run by Data Science East Africa and Lux Academy, and I will be blogging about my learning progress here.

Week 1: Introduction to Data Engineering.

Data Engineering is the design and development of systems for collecting, storing and analyzing data at scale.

Week 1 was all about familiriazing ourselves with DE concepts and setting up the environments.

Technologies used and software requirements.

  • Google Cloud Platform (GCP) a cloud-based auto-scaling platform by Google or AWS.

  • Python language for coding, a python IDE and a text editor. I prefered anaconda.

  • SQL server, MYSQL Workbench, DBeaver AND DBVisualizer.

  • Git and version control system.

Week 1 was all about basic introduction and getting started.

Can't wait to document what I learn in week 2 and hopefully get a project to work on.

item.title: 数据工程101：数据工程导论 | Data Engineering 101:Introduction to Data Engineering
item.description:
数据工程是增长最快的数据科学工作角色之一。 它是大数据的基础。 数据工程师也被称为信息架构师。

信息工程 (IE)，也称为数据工程、信息技术工程 (ITE) 或信息工程方法 (IEM)，是指构建系统以实现数据的收集和使用。 这些数据通常用于启用后续分析和数据科学； 这通常涉及机器学习。使数据可用通常涉及大量的计算和存储，以及数据处理和清理。-维基百科

本文向读者介绍了他们应该了解的基础知识，以便开始使用数据工程。 这是我之前的数据科学入门文章的延续

Objectives What is data engineering? Data engineering and big data. Data Engineers VS Data Scientists. Data pipelines. How to move and process data.
什么是数据工程？ 数据工程是指构建系统以实现数据的收集和使用。 数据工程师的主要工作是为分析或操作准备数据。 他们负责构建数据管道以汇集来自不同来源的信息。

在数据科学工作流程中，数据工程师负责数据准备、收集和存储。

数据工程师的职责

  1. 从不同来源摄取数据。

  2. 优化数据库进行分析。

  3. 删除损坏的数据。

  4. 他们开发、构建、测试和维护数据架构。

大数据
这是使用传统管理方法难以处理的大量数据。

大数据的五个V

多少钱？ 品种——数据的类型。 哪一种？ 速度 - 接收数据的速率。 多频繁？ 真实性——你的数据有多真实。 准确到什么程度？

价值 - 有用吗？

数据工程师 VS 数据科学家

  • 数据工程师收集和存储数据，数据科学家利用数据。

  • 数据工程师建立数据库，数据科学家访问数据库。

  • 数据工程师构建数据管道，数据科学家使用管道输出。

  • 数据工程师有很强的软件技能，数据科学家有很强的分析能力。

数据管道数据管道是一系列数据处理步骤：摄取 -> 处理 -> 存储。 数据管道使数据从一个站点流向另一个站点，以便数据科学家可以使用最新、准确和相关的数据。

它们确保数据的有效流动。

处理数据
处理数据是指将原始数据转换为有意义的信息。

处理数据的重要性

一个。 它有助于将数据从一种类型转换为另一种类型。 湾。 它有助于删除不需要的数据。 它有助于组织数据。 它有助于优化内存、进程和网络成本。 它有助于将数据放入结构中。

它提高了生产力。

数据工程师如何处理数据

  • 数据处理、清理和整理。

  • 将数据存储在结构合理的数据库中。

  • 在数据库表之上创建视图。

  • 优化数据库的性能。

其他资源的链接
数据工程师做什么 Coursera 文章
成为datacamp的数据工程师文章
数据工程师实际上是做什么的？
数据工程路线图

原文:

Data engineering is one of the fastest rising Data Science job roles. It is the foundation for big data. Data engineers are also known as information architects.

Information engineering (IE), also known as data engineering, information technology engineering (ITE) or information engineering methodology (IEM), refers to the building of systems to enable the collection and usage of data. This data is usually used to enable subsequent analysis and data science; which often involves machine learning.Making the data usable usually involves substantial compute and storage, as well as data processing and cleaning.-Wikipedia

This article introduces the reader to basics they should know in order to get started with data engineering. It is a continuation of my previous article getting started in data science

Objectives What is data engineering? Data engineering and big data. Data Engineers VS Data Scientists. Data pipelines. How to move and process data.
What is data engineering? Data engineering refers to the building of systems to enable the collection and usage of data. A data engineer's primary job is to prepare data for analytical or operations. They are responsible for building data pipelines to bring together information from different sources.

In the Data Science workflow, Data Engineers are responsible for data preparation, collection and storage.

Responsibilities of a data engineer

  1. Ingesting data from different sources.

  2. Optimizing databases for analysis.

  3. Removing corrupted data.

  4. They develop, construct, test and maintain data architectures.

BIG DATA
This is large volume of data that is difficult to process using traditional management methods.

The five V's of big data

Volume- The amount of data. How much? Variety- The types of data. What kind? Velocity- The rate at which data is received. How frequent? Veracity- How truthful your data is. How accurate?

Value- How useful?

Data Engineers VS Data Scientists

  • Data Engineers collect and store data, Data Scientists exploit the data.

  • Data Engineers set up databases, Data Scientists access the databases.

  • Data Engineers build data pipelines, Data Scientists use pipeline outputs.

  • Data Engineers have strong software skills, Data Scientists have strong analytical skills.

Data Pipelines A data pipeline is a series of data processing steps: Ingest ->Process ->Store. Data pipelines automate data flow from one station to the next so that Data Scientists can use up-to-date, accurate and relevant data.

They ensure efficient flow of data.

ETL and Data Pipelines ETL is a popular framework for designing data pipelines. E-Extract data T-Transform extracted data.

L-Load transformed data to another database.

Processing Data
Processing data refers to converting raw data into meaningful information.

Importance of procesing data

a. It helps to convert data from one type to another. b. It helps to remove unwanted data. c. It helps to organise data. d. It helps to optimize memory, process and network costs. e. It helps to fit data into a structure.

f. It increases productivity.

How data engineers process data

  • Data manipulation, cleaning and tidying.

  • Storing data in a sanely structured database.

  • Creating views on top of the database tables.

  • Optimizing performance of the database.

Links to other resources
What a Data Engineer does Coursera article
Becoming a data engineer article by datacamp
What do Data Engineers actually do?
Data Engineering Road Map

Benedict Nkeonye - Aug 13

Once unsuspended, torine6 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by torine6 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

item.title: 使用 GitHub Copilot 解释和翻译您的代码 | Explain and Translate Your Code With GitHub Copilot
item.description:
一个没有引起太多关注的特定功能称为 Copilot Explain。

顾名思义，Copilot Explain 会将代码翻译成自然语言描述。 对于不熟悉某个项目或编程语言的开发人员来说，这可能是一个巨大的帮助。 此功能还可以将代码从一种编程语言翻译成另一种。 当您找到不同编程语言的代码片段解决方案并需要将其翻译成存储库的目标语言时，这是一个福音。

入门要求

在所有其他情况下，您可以试用一下，看看它是否符合您的需求。 为了开始使用 GitHub Copilot，您需要一个 IDE。 对于此示例，我们将使用Visual Studio Code 。

在撰写本文时，Copilot Explain 功能处于技术预览阶段，需要您注册测试人员组。 查看GitHub Next ，了解如何参与技术预览。

在 Visual Studio Code IDE 中，为 Visual Studio Code 安装以下两个扩展：

  • 一个弹出窗口将提示您配置 Copilot。 按照设置向导将您的 GitHub 帐户连接到 GitHub Copilot。

    对于此示例，我们将模拟一种情况，您正在使用不熟悉的使用 Kotlin 编程语言的存储库。 我们将要使用的代码示例来自Kotlin 示例页面：

    如果您不熟悉 Kotlin 语法，则某些部分可能不一定立即有意义，您可能希望快速了解这段代码的作用，而无需深入了解 Kotlin 本身的复杂工作原理。

    确保在 Visual Studio Code 内的文件中提供示例代码示例，例如 example.kt。 突出显示此文件中的所有行。

    如果 Github Copilot 扩展安装正确，您的垂直活动栏中应该有一个额外的 GitHub 图标可用：

    单击 GitHub 图标后，您的侧边栏应如下所示：

    如果您还没有突出显示您的代码，请确保立即执行，然后单击名为Ask Copilot的按钮。 它将结果显示为将代码翻译成简单的英语：

    这就是您可以用简单的英语检索不熟悉的代码片段的描述的方法！ 如果您在 Kotlin 中找到此代码示例，并希望将其翻译成您熟悉的语言，GitHub Copilot 可以帮助您！ 让我们尝试将代码从 Kotlin 翻译成 Python。 单击名为Ask Copilot的蓝色按钮以翻译您的代码。 最终结果将如下所示：

    给你！ 您刚刚将一段代码从 Kotlin 翻译成 Python！ 带走

    它当然不是替代开发人员的工具。 它是一种提升开发体验的工具，就好像你旁边有人会不时地为你提供好建议一样。 这对于样板代码和重复代码特别有用。

    我们在本文中介绍的用于解释和翻译代码的新功能仍处于技术预览阶段，让我们得以一窥 AI 辅助编程的下一步可能是什么。 确保密切关注 GitHub Copilot 等工具，了解行业的下一步发展方向！ 如果内容有帮助，请随时在这里支持我：



原文:

GitHub Copilot is the new kid in town with a unique set of features that even seasoned developers can use to their advantage. One of the main selling points of GitHub Copilot is its ability to provide suggested lines of code assisted with AI. A specific feature that doesn’t get much attention is called Copilot Explain.

Copilot Explain, as the name suggests, will translate code into natural language descriptions. This can be an immense help to developers who are unfamiliar with a certain project or programming language. This feature can also translate code from one programming language to another. This is a boon for instances when you find a code snippet solution in a different programming language and need to translate it to the target language of the repository.

Requirements to get started

GitHub Copilot is free to use for students and open-source contributors. In all other cases, you can try it out with a trial to see if it fits your needs. In order to get started with GitHub Copilot, you will need an IDE. For this example, we will be using Visual Studio Code.

As of writing, the feature Copilot Explain is in technical preview and requires you to sign up for the tester group. Check out GitHub Next to see how you can be part of the technical preview.

1. Install the extensions for GitHub Copilot

Inside your Visual Studio Code IDE, install the following two extensions for Visual Studio Code:

  • GitHub Copilot
  • GitHub Copilot Labs

A popup will prompt you to configure Copilot. Follow the setup wizard to connect your GitHub account to GitHub Copilot.

2. Highlight the code to be explained

For this example, we are going to simulate a situation in which you’re working with an unfamiliar repository that uses the Kotlin programming language. Kotlin is a very modern language introduced in 2011 and it might not necessarily be a language that all developers are familiar with.

The code sample that we are going to use is from the Kotlin example page:

If you aren’t familiar with the Kotlin syntax, some parts might not necessarily make sense immediately and you might want to have a quick way to understand what this piece of code does without taking a deep dive into the intricate workings of Kotlin itself.

Make sure to have the example code sample available in a file inside Visual Studio Code as example.kt. Highlight all the lines in this file.

3. Explain the code

If the Github Copilot extensions are installed correctly, you should have an extra GitHub icon available in your vertical activity bar:

After clicking on the GitHub icon, your sidebar should like look this:

If you haven’t highlighted your code yet, make sure to do it now and then click on the button named Ask Copilot. It will show the result as a translation of the code into plain English:

So that’s how you can retrieve a description of what unfamiliar code snippet does in plain English! 🤓

4. Translate the code into a different programming language

If you found this code sample in Kotlin, and would like to translate it into a language that you are familiar with, GitHub Copilot can help you out! Let’s try to translate the code from Kotlin into Python. The GitHub Copilot activity bar has a second section called Language Translation:

Click on the blue button named Ask Copilot to translate your code. The end result will look like this:

There you go! You just translated a code snippet from Kotlin to Python! 🔎

Takeaway

GitHub Copilot can be a revolutionary code completion tool by suggesting lines of code to the developer based on a comprehensive machine learning model. It certainly isn’t a tool to replace the developer. It’s a tool to elevate the development experience as if there is someone next to you helping you out from time to time with good suggestions. This can especially be useful for boilerplate and repetitive code.

The new features that we introduced in this article for explaining and translating code are still in technical preview and gives us a glimpse into what the next step could be in AI-assisted programming. Make sure to keep an eye on tools like GitHub Copilot to see where the industry is heading next! 🚀

If the content was helpful, feel free to support me here:
item.author: duncanlew

item.title: 前 10 个很棒的 React Hooks 库 | Top 10 Awesome React Hooks Libraries
item.description:
拉维·巴蒂

发表于根据我们创建自定义钩子的用例，React 只提供了少数几个钩子。

在创建我们自己的自定义之前，我们可以查看以下对项目有帮助的反应钩子库。

用于表单状态管理和验证的 React Hooks (Web + React Native)。 它减少了您需要编写的代码量，同时消除了不必要的重新渲染。

特征

  • 构建时考虑到性能、UX 和 DX
  • 采用原生 HTML 表单验证
  • 与 UI 库的开箱即用集成
  • 体积小，无依赖
  • 支持 Yup、Zod、AJV、Superstruct、Joi、Vest、class-validator、io-ts、nope 和自定义构建

用于在 React 中获取、缓存和更新异步数据的钩子。 它具有声明性、始终最新的自动管理查询和突变，可直接改善您的开发人员和用户体验

特征

  • 传输/协议/后端不可知的数据获取（REST、GraphQL、promise 等等！）
  • 并行 + 相关查询
  • 突变 + 反应式查询重新获取
  • 多层缓存+自动垃圾收集
  • 分页 + 基于游标的查询
  • 带滚动恢复的加载更多 + 无限滚动查询
  • 取消请求
  • 专用开发工具...

用于值和回调去抖动的 React 钩子

它为应用程序生命周期的许多少数用例提供了很多钩子，以下是其中的一些： useCopyToClipboard() useDarkMode() useEventListener() useIsMounted() useMap() useSsr() useStep()等等。

它允许使用 React Hooks 创建本地状态，并仅在需要时以最小的努力将其用作 React 上下文

这是一个很棒的包，用作 Fetch API 的替代品

特征

  • 打字稿支持
  • 提供者设置默认 URL 和选项
  • 请求/响应拦截器
  • 反应原生支持
  • 组件卸载时中止/取消挂起的 HTTP 请求
  • 内置缓存
  • 持久缓存支持
  • 悬念（实验）支持
  • 重试功能

跟踪 CSS 媒体查询状态的反应传感器钩子。 由于媒体查询对于任何应用程序或网站的响应能力都非常重要，因此需要一个直接的解决方案

用于启用与本地存储同步的反应钩子。

特征

  • 自动 JSON 序列化
  • 跨多个选项卡同步
  • 跨多个选项卡同步
  • 通过 TypeScript 进行类型提示

一组有用的钩子，用于特定于某些原语类型的状态更改助手
以下是一些 Hooks： useInput useBoolean useNumber useArray useOnMount useOnUnmount等等...

奖金

  • 结论

    它们为我们提供了多种用途的多种选择，因此我们不必从头开始构建它们。

    还有更多的钩子库，我只选择了最流行和维护的，如果我错过了任何好的库，请在评论部分写下来。

    谢谢你的阅读..!!

    学分

深夜编码器 - 一旦暂停，ravics1721 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ravics1721 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有 ravics1721 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ravics1721 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 ravics1721 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ravics1721 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ravi Bharti

Posted on Aug 21

Hooks in React has been life-changing for React developers they provide a simple elegant way to write code that is more readable and maintainable. There are only a few handfuls of hooks provided by React depending on our use case we create custom hooks.

Before creating our own custom we can look up to following react hooks libraries that help in the project.

1. React Hooks Form

Github Docs

React Hooks for form state management and validation (Web + React Native). It reduces the amount of code you need to write while removing unnecessary re-renders.

Features

  • Built with performance, UX, and DX in mind
  • Embraces native HTML form validation
  • Out-of-the-box integration with UI libraries
  • Small size and no dependencies
  • Support Yup, Zod, AJV, Superstruct, Joi, Vest, class-validator, io-ts, nope, and custom build

2. React Use

Github Docs

React use has a collection of essential React Hooks for managing devices sensors, UI controls, animations, side-effects, Lifecycle, State, and more

3. React Query

Github Docs

Hooks for fetching, caching, and updating asynchronous data in React. It has declarative, always-up-to-date auto-managed queries and mutations that directly improve both your developer and user experiences

Features

  • Transport/protocol/backend agnostic data fetching (REST, GraphQL, promises, whatever!)
  • Auto Caching + Refetching (stale-while-revalidate, Window Refocus, Polling/Realtime)
  • Parallel + Dependent Queries
  • Mutations + Reactive Query Refetching
  • Multi-layer Cache + Automatic Garbage Collection
  • Paginated + Cursor-based Queries
  • Load-More + Infinite Scroll Queries w/ Scroll Recovery
  • Request Cancellation
  • React Suspense + Fetch-As-You-Render Query Prefetching
  • Dedicated Devtools ...

4. useDebounce

Github

React hook for value and callback debouncing

5. useHooks-ts

Github Docs

It has a lot of hooks for many of the handful use cases of the application lifecycle, Here are some of the following: useCopyToClipboard() useDarkMode() useEventListener() useIsMounted() useMap() useSsr() useStep() and many more.

6. Constate

Github

It let create local states using React Hooks and use it as a React Context only when needed with minimum effort

7. useHttp

Github Docs

It is an awesome package that is used as the replacement of Fetch API

Features

  • SSR (server-side rendering) support
  • TypeScript support
  • 2 dependencies (use-ssr, urs)
  • GraphQL support (queries + mutations)
  • Provider to set default URL and options
  • Request/response interceptors
  • React Native support
  • Aborts/Cancels pending HTTP requests when a component unmounts
  • Built in caching
  • Persistent caching support
  • Suspense(experimental) support
  • Retry functionality

8. useMedia

Github

React sensor hook that tracks the state of a CSS media query. It takes a straightforward solution to the issue as media queries are extremely critical for the responsiveness of any application or website

9. rehooks/local-storage

Github Docs
React hook for enabling synchronization with local storage.

Features

  • Automatic JSON serialization
  • Synchronization across multiple tabs
  • Synchronization across multiple tabs
  • Type hinting via TypeScript

10. React Hanger

Github

Set of helpful hooks, for specific to some primitives types state changing helpers
react-hanger is a library that has some React Hooks that let us more easily manage various kinds of states. Here are some following Hooks: useInput useBoolean useNumber useArray useOnMount useOnUnmount and many more...

Bonus

  • awesome-react-hooks A curated resource of react hooks
  • **React Recieps A React Hooks utility library containing popular customized hooks
  • component-size React hook for determining the size of a component
  • online-status React hook for subscribing to online/offline events and the navigator.onLine property to see the current status

Conclusion

Hooks are reusable, clean, and simple functions that can be incorporated into your workflow. They offer us many options for a diverse range of uses, so we don’t have to build them from scratch.

There are also many more hooks libraries out there, I have only selected which are most popular and maintained, if I have missed any good libraries, write them down in the comment section.

Thank you for reading..!!

Credits

Late Night Coder - Aug 15

Once suspended, ravics1721 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ravics1721 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ravics1721 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ravics1721 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ravics1721:

ravics1721 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ravics1721 will restore default visibility to their posts.
